rationale: Ground truth contains no substantive concerns (only LGTM/approval), so recall is trivially maxed for both. X performs deeper source-level verification (tracing actual code paths in serving_chat.py and the TTS talker module) but produces only one actionable comment, and that comment is heavily hedged ('if you observed silent WAV directly, keep the current wording'), reducing its actionability. Y verifies claims via repo-wide greps and additionally catches a genuinely valuable out-of-diff issue — a sibling doc (examples/online_serving/minicpmo/README.md:58) left with the same stale extra_body pattern this PR fixes — plus gives concrete suggested rewordings with file:line for all three findings, though one nit slightly overclaims 'deterministic' behavior from a single test run. Y's extra grounded finding and more concrete, actionable suggestions edge out X's greater verification depth but thinner actionable output.
